description: Security audit trail for installed agent skills. Each entry records when a skill was reviewed, the verdict, and any findings.

audits.notes: Adapted from vercel-labs/skills. Source is trusted. No tool permissions requested beyond npx skills CLI and WebFetch/WebSearch for skill discovery.
Authored for this base project. Read-only analysis skill — no tool permissions beyond reading files and searching content. No external network access.

Crash-proof file-based planning methodology (task_plan.md, findings.md, progress.md). 60+ agents supported. Pure markdown methodology — no data exfiltration patterns, no network calls, no suspicious instructions. Tools scoped to project-local Read/Write/Bash. Strong community trust (23K stars, 2K forks).

Pre-coding clarification skill. Relentlessly interviews user about plans/designs until all decision branches are resolved. Pure instructional content — no tool declarations beyond AskUserQuestion/Read. Matt Pocock is a trusted TypeScript educator and Vercel alumni. 399K installs on skills.sh.

Strict red-green-refactor TDD methodology. Tests only public interfaces (no coupling to internals). Includes anti-pattern reference. Pure instructional content — no network calls, no exfiltration, no suspicious patterns. Well-scoped: references companion files (tests.md, mocking.md) rather than bloating the main SKILL.md.

Architectural improvement scanner. Generates HTML reports of deepening opportunities. Uses Read for analysis and Write for report generation — no network access, no exfiltration. Designed for local project use only.

Dependency supply chain risk assessment from Trail of Bits — industry-leading security firm (audited Kubernetes, Ethereum, etc.). CC-BY-SA-4.0 license. Professional security tooling — well-scoped, reputable source.
Security-focused PR/diff analysis from Trail of Bits. Finds vulnerabilities traditional code review misses. Complements the project's security-conscious posture.
Official Anthropic skill. Production-grade frontend interfaces with distinctive, non-generic AI aesthetics. Maximum source trust. Recommended as optional for projects with UI.

Complete 7-stage engineering methodology (brainstorming → git-worktrees → writing-plans → subagent-driven-dev → TDD → verification → code-review → finish). MIT License. 204K stars, 680K+ installs. Very opinionated — recommended as optional for teams wanting maximum process discipline, not as default. 15 individual skills included; heavyweight install.

Complements grill-me: 5-step PRD creation (problem exploration → codebase review → stakeholder interview → module sketching → document generation). Outputs local issues/prd.md only — explicitly instructs 'Do NOT submit a GitHub issue or call any external service.' No network access, no exfiltration patterns. All three security scanners (Gen Agent Trust Hub, Socket, Snyk) report Pass. Matt Pocock's ai-engineer-workshop-2026-project repo with 164 stars. Originally forked from mattpocock/skills (trusted source in this audit).

Decomposes a PRD into independently-grabbable issues using vertical slices (tracer bullets) — each issue cuts through ALL integration layers end-to-end, never horizontal slices of a single layer. 3-step process: locate PRD → explore codebase → draft vertical slices. Outputs local markdown files only. All three security scanners report Pass. Same trusted repo as write-a-prd.

Lighter complement to differential-review from Trail of Bits — industry-leading security firm (audited Kubernetes, Ethereum, etc.). Evaluates API/config/interface design for dangerous defaults where the 'easy path' leads to insecurity. Focuses on security ergonomics, not implementation bugs. CC-BY-SA-4.0 license. Gen Agent Trust Hub: Pass, Snyk: Pass. Socket scanner shows 'Warn' but this is likely a false positive given Trail of Bits' reputation as a top-tier security shop. Complements supply-chain-risk-auditor and differential-review in the security tier.

Relentless interview that maintains CONTEXT.md and ADRs. SKILL.md is a 3-line wrapper delegating to /grilling and /domain-modeling sub-skills (same trusted repo). All three auto-scanners (Gen/Socket/Snyk) report Safe/0 alerts/Low Risk. Only finding: sub-skill dependencies not individually installed — mitigated by same-repo trust.
findings: Dependency chain: references /grilling and /domain-modeling sub-skills which were not installed individually. These are in the same mattpocock/skills repo. Mitigation: use grill-me methodology directly if sub-skills unavailable.

audits.notes: Official DuckDB spatial extension skill — part of duckdb/duckdb-skills repo. Teaches agents to answer spatial questions using DuckDB's spatial extension and Overture Maps (free global data). Only uses local DuckDB binary + public S3 data. Delegates to install-duckdb (same repo) if binary missing. Clean, well-structured, no security concerns.

Official Mapbox skill — part of mapbox/mapbox-agent-skills repo (19 skills). Documents integration patterns for Mapbox MCP Server. 100% instructional content: no external calls, no tool declarations, no executable code. References are local files in the same repo. Mapbox is an established company; the MCP server is their official product. Relevant for geospatial operations (distance, area, isochrones, routing).

CRITICAL: The listed source repo (postgis/postgis) is the actual PostGIS C source code — it contains NO SKILL.md files and no skills/ directory. GitHub Code Search returned zero results for 'postgis-skill SKILL.md'. Either the skill was removed, the skills.sh listing is incorrect, or the skill lives in an undocumented fork/repo. Cannot verify the skill content. REJECT until actual source is identified.
findings: Source not found: postgis/postgis repo has no SKILL.md. Skills.sh listing appears to reference incorrect or outdated source.
Unable to verify any of the 6 security dimensions — skill content is inaccessible.
